context: BTC/USD trades at $75,653 (+0.30% 24h) as April closes with spot ETF inflows of $2.4B — the strongest institutional demand month of 2026. The FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% (Apr 29, 8-4 vote) with Powell's chairmanship ending May 15 and crypto-friendly Kevin Warsh set to assume the role. XRP leads alt sentiment as the CLARITY Act Senate Banking Committee markup window expires; 120+ firms formally urged passage. ETH consolidates post-Pectra hard fork at $2,268. Total crypto market cap sits at $2.6T (-1.5% 24h). BTC dominance 59.9%. NFP (May 1) and PCE (today) are the binary macro triggers for the week — inline/soft prints would extend the risk-on crypto rally into May.

tickers.name: XRP / USD
tickers.thesis: CLARITY Act Senate Banking Committee markup window closes end of April — binary catalyst for XRP's legal classification as a digital commodity. 120+ firms publicly urged Senate passage; SEC/CFTC Project Crypto infrastructure already built. Failure to floor-vote by May effectively shelves the bill for 2026. Price consolidating $1.35-$1.40 after week-long pullback; structure still bullish above $1.28.

tickers.name: BTC / USD
tickers.thesis: Spot ETF inflows $2.4B in April — strongest month of 2026. BlackRock IBIT holds ~812K BTC (~$62B AUM). FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% (Apr 29); Powell's final meeting amid growing dissent (8-4 vote). Incoming Chair Warsh viewed as crypto-friendly. BTC up ~12% MTD but cooling off $77.4K highs — watching $73.5K OTE retracement for continuation entry.

tickers.name: ETH / USD
tickers.thesis: Post-Pectra upgrade consolidation — ETH jumped 20%+ on the hard fork but is now digesting gains. 32% of supply staked (up from 24% two years ago). Bitmine Immersion accumulating 5.08M ETH (~$13.3B) targeting 5% of total supply. Glamsterdam fork (H1 2026) approaching on roadmap. ETH/BTC ratio compressed; needs reclaim of 0.030 for meaningful outperformance. Bias neutral until clear breakout above $2,346 or retest of $2,150 OTE.

insights.title: XRP: CLARITY Act is the binary trade
insights.body: The Senate Banking Committee window closes end of April. Three coordinated endorsements (Armstrong/Coinbase, Treasury Sec. Bessent, SEC Chair Atkins) landed in early April, removing the primary industry obstacle. If the bill reaches the Senate floor before May, XRP digital-commodity classification is effectively locked in federal law — analysts forecast $5-10 by late 2026. Failure to advance kills the bill for 2026. Position: small long above $1.35 SSL; full size on confirmed markup headline.

insights.title: BTC: ETF inflow momentum through May
insights.body: April saw $2.4B in spot ETF inflows (strongest month of 2026); BlackRock IBIT now holds ~812K BTC worth ~$62B. Four consecutive weeks of net positive flows signal institutional accumulation. The OTE zone $73,500-$74,800 is the NY killzone entry target for Tuesday/Wednesday sessions. Watch $77,410 BSL sweep as first target — clean daily candle above re-opens $80K psychological BSL.

insights.title: FOMC + PCE today: key binary for crypto direction
insights.body: Fed held 3.50-3.75% yesterday (8-4 dissent); PCE and GDP print today at 07:30 ET. Markets pricing 2.8% PCE — inline is neutral. A soft GDP read (<1.0%) would increase rate-cut odds and pump crypto into NFP Friday.
